column1: # grep-tells.tsv — TIER 1 (flat-presence) rules for audit-swiftui-uikit-overuse (over-01 … over-07).
# severity: hard | warn | adv   (hard → runner exits 2). The runner greps each ERE over the target *.swift.
# This LOCATES only — the LLM agent READS each hit (SKILL.md step 3) and applies the WHETHER test: a
# class name is NOT a verdict (a UITextField bridge may be a justified inputAccessoryView hatch). The
# structural tell grep can't express (a makeUIView that CONSTRUCTS a trivial control) is in

column4: 1:1-native UIKit control — if wrapped in UIViewRepresentable for plain use, a SwiftUI control fits (over-01). READ: confirm it is not a justified first-responder/inputAccessoryView/custom hatch.
UIScreen.main(.bounds) read for layout — deprecated iOS 16+; use GeometryReader/containerRelativeFrame (iOS 17+)/horizontalSizeClass (over-02). cross_ref adaptive-layout.
UIApplication.shared.windows/.keyWindow to reach the active scene — use @Environment(\.scenePhase)/UIWindowScene via the scene delegate (over-03). cross_ref app-lifecycle-background.
UIPasteboard bridge — for a model type prefer PasteButton (iOS 16+)/.copyable + Transferable (over-04). cross_ref document-picker-permissions.
UIKit blur/glass bridged — use SwiftUI .glassEffect(_:in:) (iOS 26)/Material (.ultraThinMaterial, iOS 15) (over-05). cross_ref liquid-glass, appearance-color.
reverse bridge (SwiftUI-in-UIKit) — in a SwiftUI-first app a whole-screen host may mean a SwiftUI navigation scene fits (over-06). READ to confirm scope. cross_ref adaptive-navigation.

UITextView bridge — if plain/lightly-styled text AND floor is iOS 26, TextEditor(text:selection:) may remove it (over-07); below 26 (or true rich text/inputAccessoryView) it is a JUSTIFIED hatch — do not flag.
